selection: This connector publishes a curated, exhaustive (within declared scope) slice of the Census Bureau's public JSON API. We cover the programs whose data is widely referenced in economic and demographic analysis and that are available via https://api.census.gov/data.json with stable vintaged endpoints. Programs not exposed on that API (e.g. Building Permits Survey, decennial PUMS microdata, internal-use files) are intentionally excluded.
refresh_strategy: On each run we fetch the Census data.json catalog and compute a fingerprint over (identifier, modified) pairs. If the fingerprint matches the prior run, catalog-dependent work short-circuits. Otherwise, each program node enumerates its in-scope (program, vintage) entries, fetches variables.json for labels, pulls the data with chunked requests, and merges the result keyed on (vintage|time, geo_level, state_fips, county_fips, [measure]) so the published asset accumulates across years.

us_census_catalog.title: US Census Bureau Dataset Registry
us_census_catalog.description: The subset of the Census Bureau's public API catalog (https://api.census.gov/data.json) that this connector ingests, one row per (program, vintage) actually pulled into a published dataset. Use it to see which vintages and endpoints back each us_census_* asset.

column_descriptions.subset_id: Published asset this entry contributes to (e.g. us_census_acs)
column_descriptions.program: Census program code (acs, cbp, pep, saipe, sahie, intltrade, ecnbasic)
column_descriptions.path: Census API dataset path (c_dataset joined with /)
column_descriptions.vintage: Catalog vintage year (integer); null for non-vintaged timeseries programs
column_descriptions.title: Official Census Bureau title for this (path, vintage) entry
column_descriptions.api_endpoint: Full https URL used to fetch data rows
column_descriptions.variables_link: URL of the variables.json describing every variable in this entry
column_descriptions.modified: Census-reported last-modified timestamp for this catalog entry
column_descriptions.identifier: Stable Census identifier URL for this entry

us_census_acs.title: American Community Survey 1-Year Detailed Tables
us_census_acs.description: Curated slice of the ACS 1-Year Detailed Tables, spanning state and county geographies across every available vintage from 2010 to the latest release. We pull only estimate variables (names ending in 'E', excluding margin-of-error and annotation columns) that belong to one of 20 hand-picked Summary File tables covering age, sex, race, Hispanic origin, educational attainment, household income, poverty, SNAP, employment status, occupation, commute, housing, tenure, and rent. Variables are fetched in chunks of 49 per request to stay under the Census API's 50-variable limit, so no tables are silently truncated. Output is in long format: one row per (vintage, geo_level, state_fips, county_fips, variable).

column_descriptions.county_fips: 3-digit FIPS county code within state; empty string for state-level rows
column_descriptions.label: Variable label from the Census variables metadata endpoint
column_descriptions.table_id: Summary File table group the variable belongs to (e.g. B01001)
column_descriptions.value: Raw string value returned by the API
column_descriptions.value_numeric: Numeric parse of value, null where non-numeric or suppressed

us_census_cbp.title: County Business Patterns: State Totals by NAICS
us_census_cbp.description: County Business Patterns at the state level, broken down by NAICS industry code. For each available vintage from 2012 onward we pull establishments, employment, annual payroll, and Q1 payroll across every NAICS sector the API exposes (2-digit through 6-digit). The NAICS dimension version comes from the API's variables.json — NAICS2012 for vintage 2012 and NAICS2017 for vintages 2017-present. Published with vintage, state, and NAICS as a composite key so multiple years coexist in a single table. County-level CBP is intentionally out of scope for this release because the per-county NAICS-detail responses are an order of magnitude larger; that's a follow-up.

column_descriptions.naics: NAICS industry code (string; '00' = all sectors). Code system is whichever NAICS vintage the API uses for that CBP year.
column_descriptions.naics_label: Human-readable industry description from the API

us_census_pep.title: Population Estimates Program
us_census_pep.description: Population estimates at state and county level from the Population Estimates Program. We pull from two pep/* sub-endpoints — pep/population (POP, DENSITY and per-year POP_YYYY/DENSITY_YYYY variants) and pep/components (BIRTHS, DEATHS, NATURALINC, DOMESTICMIG, INTERNATIONALMIG, NETMIG and rate variants) — for every vintage from 2015 onward where the API exposes them. Character-breakdown paths (pep/charage, pep/charagegroups, pep/subcty) are excluded because they use different geography hierarchies. The schema of PEP variables changes between vintages (e.g. 2019 has POP/DENSITY; 2021 splits POP into POP_2020/POP_2021), so the output is in long format: one row per (vintage, path, geography, variable). The variable name and human-readable label from the Census variables metadata are preserved, with a numeric parse of the reported value. POPGROUP, AGE, HISP and other dimension/index codes are not emitted as rows — only measurement variables are kept.

column_descriptions.vintage: PEP vintage year (the release year of the estimate)
column_descriptions.path: Census API path the data came from (pep/population, pep/components, or pep/housing)
column_descriptions.county_fips: 3-digit FIPS county code; empty string for state-level rows
column_descriptions.variable: PEP variable name (e.g. POP, DENSITY, POP_2021, BIRTHS)
column_descriptions.label: Variable label from the Census variables metadata endpoint
column_descriptions.value: Raw string value returned by the API
column_descriptions.value_numeric: Numeric parse of value; null if non-numeric

us_census_saipe.title: Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates
us_census_saipe.description: Model-based estimates of income and poverty for US states and counties from the SAIPE timeseries API (timeseries/poverty/saipe). We pull every year available from 2005 forward for all poverty-rate and median-household-income measures, at both state and county geographies. Output merges year, geography, and measure into a single long-form table; re-running the connector incrementally picks up the next release.

column_descriptions.county_fips: 3-digit FIPS county code; empty string for state-level rows
column_descriptions.saepovall_pt: Estimated number of people of all ages in poverty
column_descriptions.saepovrtall_pt: Estimated poverty rate (%) for all ages
column_descriptions.saepov0_17_pt: Estimated number of people ages 0-17 in poverty
column_descriptions.saepov5_17r_pt: Estimated number of people ages 5-17 in families in poverty
column_descriptions.saepovrt5_17r_pt: Estimated poverty rate (%) for ages 5-17 in families

us_census_sahie.title: Small Area Health Insurance Estimates
us_census_sahie.description: Model-based estimates of health insurance coverage at state and county level from the SAHIE timeseries API (timeseries/healthins/sahie). We pull every year available from 2008 forward. Output keeps demographic breakdown categories (AGECAT, RACECAT, SEXCAT, IPRCAT) as filter parameters set to the 'all' code so each row is the population total for that geography-year; detailed breakdowns are out of scope for this release.

column_descriptions.county_fips: 3-digit FIPS county code; empty string for state-level rows
column_descriptions.nipr_pt: Estimated total population in the income-to-poverty ratio category

us_census_intltrade_annual.title: US International Trade: Annual Imports and Exports by End-Use
us_census_intltrade_annual.description: Annual US international trade totals at the one-digit End-Use commodity level (COMM_LVL=EU1), compiled from the Census monthly timeseries APIs timeseries/intltrade/imports/enduse and timeseries/intltrade/exports/enduse. We query each endpoint at time=YYYY-12 and read the year-to-date columns (GEN_VAL_YR, CON_VAL_YR for imports; ALL_VAL_YR for exports) to obtain full calendar-year totals in a single request per year per flow. Coverage is every full year from 2013 to the latest year for which the Census has published December data. Output is long format keyed on (year, flow, enduse, measure).

column_descriptions.measure: Value measure: 'general_imports' or 'consumption_imports' for imports; 'total_exports' for exports
column_descriptions.value_usd: Year-to-date value for the measure, in US dollars

us_census_ecnbasic.title: Economic Census: Summary Statistics
us_census_ecnbasic.description: Summary statistics from the US Economic Census (ecnbasic endpoint) for vintages 2017 and 2022, at the national level broken down by NAICS industry. We pull establishment count, employment, annual payroll, and total receipts for every NAICS code the API exposes. The Economic Census runs every five years, so the connector will pick up the 2027 vintage automatically once it lands in the catalog.

column_descriptions.naics: NAICS industry code (string). The NAICS vintage matches the Census vintage: 2017 uses NAICS2017, 2022 uses NAICS2022.
